ground_truth.title: Hyper-Local Regolith Binding Agent Optimization via Multi-Modal Data Fusion and Bayesian Reinforcement Learning for Martian Concrete Construction (RBC-MRCC)
ground_truth.main_content: This research proposes a novel methodology for optimizing the performance of Martian concrete utilizing in-situ regolith aggregates. Addressing the challenge of inconsistent regolith composition across the Martian surface, we leverage multi-modal data ingestion, advanced parsing techniques, and Bayesian Reinforcement Learning (BRL) to dynamically adjust a hyper-local binding agent formulation. The resulting HyperScore, a metric quantifying concrete structural integrity and durability, guides autonomous optimization towards construction-grade material production, ensuring consistent quality regardless of regolith variability. This approach directly addresses the critical bottleneck in Martian habitat construction, offering a scalable and adaptable pathway to in-situ resource utilization (ISRU). The RBC-MRCC framework comprises six key modules: Multi-modal Data Ingestion & Normalization Layer, Semantic & Structural Decomposition Module, Multi-layered Evaluation Pipeline (Logical Consistency Engine, Formula & Code Verification Sandbox, Novelty & Originality Analysis, Impact Forecasting, Reproducibility & Feasibility Scoring), Meta-Self-Evaluation Loop, Score Fusion & Weight Adjustment Module, and Human-AI Hybrid Feedback Loop. The BRL agent operates within a discrete action space, utilizing a Gaussian Process Prior and Thompson Sampling strategy to select optimal binding agent formulations. The HyperScore formula incorporates LogicScore, Novelty, Impact Forecast, Reproducibility, and Meta-stability metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that dynamically optimized formulations exhibited higher compressive strength, improved durability under simulated Martian radiation, and more predictable performance compared to fixed binding agent formulations.
with: This research proposes a novel methodology for optimizing the performance of Martian concrete utilizing in-situ regolith aggregates.

Addressing the challenge of inconsistent regolith composition across the Martian surface, we leverage multi-modal data ingestion, advanced parsing techniques, and Bayesian Reinforcement Learning (BRL) to dynamically adjust a hyper-local binding agent formulation.
The RBC-MRCC framework represents a paradigm shift in Martian concrete production, moving beyond fixed formulations to dynamically adapting binding agent recipes.
The BRL agent utilizes a Thompson Sampling strategy to select actions, balancing exploration and exploitation.
The key finding is the RBC-MRCC framework consistently produced higher-quality concrete across varying simulated regolith compositions compared to using fixed binding agent formulations.
